description: Fix Router workflow timeout caused by non-existent Bot Trigger target

context.summary: Router workflow (L94Ziar3GQZLUU1V) forwards to Echo + Bot Trigger. Bot Trigger uses custom nodes not installed in n8n.

tasks: Option A: Remove Bot Trigger forward from Router workflow (quick fix)
Option B: Deploy custom whatsapp-n8n-nodes to production n8n - DONE
Update Router workflow in n8n UI or via database - URL already correct
Test Router workflow no longer times out - Bot Trigger works, Echo times out
verification: POST /webhook/whatsapp/router returns within 2s (not 10-15s) - PARTIAL: Bot Trigger works, Echo still slow
Incoming WhatsApp messages processed without delay - PARTIAL
Router results show all targets successful - NOT YET: aggregation issue

notes.root_cause: Router forwards to /webhook/df4ce89f-fe74-4d2b-8146-2c47b69f0262/webhook which uses CUSTOM.whatsAppBotTrigger node not installed
notes.affected_workflow: Testing Trigger and Action Node (5hyhJ1ublFP7Ze6SAnV21) - NOW ACTIVE with webhook registered
notes.remaining_issue: Echo workflow uses responseNode mode, times out when wwebjs-api is slow

completed: docker-compose.override.yml created with volume mount (git-tracked file unchanged)
Bot Trigger webhook now responds (returns firstEntryJson)
Testing Trigger workflow activates on n8n startup
Local deploy/n8n/ folder mirrors server structure for documentation
follow_up_tasks: Echo workflow responseNode mode causes slow response (separate task)
Router Aggregate Results parallel execution issue (separate task)
